validation_cycles.overview: The HLD agent performs self-validation before completion. These checks ensure traceability, no overlap, appropriate sizing, and testable acceptance criteria. No external validator is invoked — the agent self-validates and iterates until all checks pass.

check.name: traceability_check
check.description: Every HLD section traces to a user story; every user story traces to a PRD requirement.

procedure.step: For each HLD section, verify parent linkage to a user story and PRD section.
For each user story, verify mapping to PRD section 7 user story groups.

check.failure_action: Add traceability or remove orphaned content before completion.

check.name: overlap_check
check.description: No sibling overlap between user stories or between HLD sections.

procedure.step: Compare scope boundaries of each user story pair.
Compare scope boundaries of each HLD section pair.

check.failure_action: Require explicit boundary rewrite to eliminate overlap before completion.

check.name: sizing_check
check.description: Each HLD section is sized for one implementation cycle.

procedure.step: For each HLD section, assess whether it could be implemented in one focused cycle.
If a section is too broad, split it into smaller sections.

check.failure_action: Split oversized HLD sections before completion.

check.name: acceptance_criteria_testability_check
check.description: Every acceptance criterion is testable and observable.

procedure.step: For each acceptance criterion in user stories and HLD sections, ask: "Can you write a test for this right now?"
Flag any criterion that uses vague terms: "correctly", "intuitive", "user-friendly", "works well".

check.failure_action: Rewrite vague criteria with specific observable conditions before completion.

check.name: coverage_check
check.description: All PRD requirements have corresponding user stories and HLD coverage.

procedure.step: Map each PRD section 7 user story group to at least one user story.
Map each architecture component to at least one HLD section.
Map each security requirement (if plan/security.md exists) to HLD or user story.

check.failure_action: Add missing user stories or HLD sections before completion.

steps.step: If any check fails, iterate and re-run before writing final artifacts.
